mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 220 in the CPIAUCSL index signals central bank inaction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as real rates rise and equity valuations contract over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite CPIAUCSL > 220, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE signal a tightening labor market, boosting corporate earnings and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) or 20d returns are negative in > 50% of interaction cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand, pushing equity prices higher as corporate revenues expand, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined GDPC1 and HOUST signals fail to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) or 60d returns are negative in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing consumer spending (RSAFS) directly boosts corporate revenues, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants price in stronger demand.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances despite RSAFS growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% signals labor market tightness, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances despite UNRATE < 5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates robust supply-side growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns as corporate margins expand over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint effect of INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) or 20d returns are negative in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) during high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 220) signals aggressive monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns as discount rates rise and risk appetite declines over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 2% and CPIAUCSL > 220, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
